title: Standardize --depth 50 across all clone-create sites; locate and explicitly clone-create analyst-baby's missing clone source
related_directives: DIRECTIVE-20260508-001 (sonnet-poller audit + /tmp clone cleanup empowerment — sibling clone-discipline concern)
related_props: PROP-016 (Mech A NEVER_PUSH detect — sibling clone-discipline concern)
PROP-034 (split-analyst baby — created the analyst-baby agent whose clone source is the puzzle this PROP partly investigates)
related_docs: CLAUDE.md 'Per-session clone discipline' (2026-05-09) — operator-side discipline section anchoring this work
scope_disclaimer: Tinker design-only per DIRECTIVE-20260518-002 out_of_scope: 'Do NOT self-apply any prompt edit'. Operator + cowork-claude apply. Six surgical patches (or three, depending on operator preference on baby-clone — see counter_args Q3) plus FUSE-staleness footnote.

executive_summary.what_changed_since_directive_filed: Between directive filing (2026-05-18T06:45Z) and this audit (2026-05-20T02:55Z), the operator-direct in-session patches to 4 files (curmudgeon.md, workspace-sync.md, decider-patches-and-selfapply.md, OPERATOR-RUNBOOK.md) have all LANDED on GitHub HEAD but are STALE in the FUSE workspace. So the audit must distinguish (a) the canonical state agents see when cloning fresh (GitHub HEAD — patched) from (b) what tinker sees reading FUSE (un-patched). All claims below are anchored to GitHub HEAD via curl verification.
executive_summary.scope_summary: Found 8 total clone-create sites across monitor/prompts/. 7 of 8 already have --depth 50 on GitHub HEAD (4 from operator-direct 2026-05-18 + 3 from PROPs 038/039/041). 1 remaining un-patched site (workspace-sync.md line 581 — strand-rescue recipe inside JSON template). Plus 2 implicit sites: analyst.md Step 0a and analyst-baby.md Step 0a only refresh-existing, never clone-create, yet baby clearly had a 659MB clone — the root cause is not in any prompt file. This PROP recommends adding an explicit clone-create-with-depth branch to analyst.md Step 0a to close the implicit-clone mystery defensively.
executive_summary.patch_count_explanation: Patch P1 covers workspace-sync.md:581 (strand-rescue text). Patch P2 covers analyst.md Step 0a (add clone-create with --depth 50 in the else branch). Patch P3 covers analyst-baby.md Step 0a inheritance text (point at the new analyst.md behavior). Patch P4 is a CLAUDE.md cross-reference update so the OWNED_BY operator-discipline section calls out the standard depth value.
executive_summary.operator_decision_required: Q3 (counter_arguments_addressed) — accept the baby implicit-clone defensive fix, or punt to a separate investigation? P2/P3 are independent of P1 so the operator can apply P1 alone first and defer P2/P3.

audit_results.method: Exhaustive grep across monitor/prompts/, monitor/prompts/reference/, monitor/scripts/, build-scripts/ for `git clone` literal. Each hit cross-referenced against GitHub HEAD via curl (because the FUSE workspace is stale for 4 of these files — the operator-applied edits from 2026-05-18 never round-tripped back to FUSE; see FUSE-staleness footnote below).

STALE — FUSE workspace line 25 missing the --depth 50 flag
STALE — FUSE workspace line 71 missing the --depth 50 flag
STALE — FUSE workspace line 14 missing the --depth 50 flag
MATCH (this file was created with --depth 50; no operator-direct edit in 2026-05-18 batch)

sites_needing_depth_50.agent: operator (strand-rescue recipe inside heredoc'd JSON template emitted into monitor/decisions/never-push-strand-*.json)
sites_needing_depth_50.rationale_for_inclusion: Operator-facing rescue recipe. When the operator follows it, they get a full clone for ONE-TIME use (copy files in, push, throw away). They never need deep history for the rescue. --depth 50 reduces clone time + disk pressure during operator recovery — exactly when the system is already under pressure.

sites_needing_depth_50.line_gh: Step 0a, lines 41-55 (the if/else branch around CLEAN_CLONE)
sites_needing_depth_50.current_state_gh: if [ -d "${CLEAN_CLONE}/.git" ]; then git pull --rebase; else echo 'skipping (first run or ephemeral session)'; fi
sites_needing_depth_50.rationale_for_inclusion: Step 0a refreshes an existing clone but does NOT create one when the path is empty. Yet baby's 2026-05-18T05:14Z run summary reported a 659MB FULL clone existed. The clone is materializing without any explicit clone-create command in any prompt file. This PROP recommends a DEFENSIVE FIX: explicitly clone-with-depth-50 in the else branch, so the clone source is documented and depth-bounded regardless of whatever implicit mechanism currently creates it. See baby_clone_source section for the root-cause investigation.

sites_needing_depth_50.current_state_gh: Bullet text: 'Step 0a — Refresh the clean clone (Phase 1 Change 1.5 procedure)' — pointing at analyst.md without restating the procedure
sites_needing_depth_50.rationale_for_inclusion: If P2 changes analyst.md Step 0a from refresh-only to refresh-or-clone-with-depth-50, the inheritance reference in baby's philosophy section should reflect the new behavior. Tiny clarification — one-word update from 'Refresh' to 'Refresh-or-create'.

sites_needing_depth_50.line_gh: Per-session clone discipline section (~line 145, the bullet that mentions 'Use --depth 50 ...' if any)
sites_needing_depth_50.current_state_gh: CLAUDE.md per-session clone discipline section is operator-discipline-focused, NOT prompt-file-focused. It does not currently state '--depth 50 is the project standard' — the operator-direct edits made --depth 50 the de-facto standard but never wrote it down as a convention.
sites_needing_depth_50.rationale_for_inclusion: Make --depth 50 a stated convention so future PROPs / future operator-direct edits / future agents creating clone-sites don't have to re-derive the value. Brief sentence add: 'When adding a new clone-create command anywhere in the project, use --depth 50.'

out_of_scope_sites_no_clone.note: No clone-create. Tinker reads from FUSE; Mode 1-4 procedures never spawn a clone. Tinker's only relationship to clones is the /tmp/*-clone cleanup empowerment (line 192-202) — a `rm -rf` not a `git clone`. No patch needed.

Narrative-only mention of /tmp/dome-integrity-clone — the prose says 'if you need a fresh clone for this check, use /tmp/dome-integrity-clone' but does not contain an actual `git clone` command. Integrity agent reads from FUSE in practice. No patch needed — but a follow-up could surface a clone-create command and patch it. See out_of_scope_followups item 2.
Comment-only — JS file says 'WARNING: this only prunes the git clone. The FUSE workspace mount cannot ...' No actual `git clone` invocation. No patch needed.
No git-clone invocations in any of audit-narrative-citations.js, audit-rewrite.js, backfill-expansion-tracker-archive.js, compute-drift-audit.js, verify-pending-state.js. Verified via grep.

baby_clone_source.investigation_summary: DIRECTIVE-20260518-002 task_2 asks: 'Figure out where analyst-baby actually obtains its clone.' The 2026-05-18T05:14Z baby run reported a 659MB clone existed for cleanup; operator-direct grep before this audit could not find a clone-create command anywhere in baby's reachable prompts.
what_was_searched: monitor/prompts/analyst-baby.md — full file (199 lines, FUSE = GitHub HEAD; verified diff'd to empty). Has Step 0a inheritance bullet → analyst.md Step 0a. Has Cleanup section (lines 162-200) which references CLEAN_CLONE for an `rm -rf` but does not contain a `git clone` command.
monitor/prompts/analyst.md Step 0a (lines 37-58). Logic: if .git exists → fetch+pull-rebase; else → echo 'skipping'. No clone-create.
monitor/prompts/analyst.md all other steps (Mode 0-5, Postlude). Verified no git clone command anywhere in the 370-line file.

All monitor/prompts/reference/ — none of analyst-mode1-expansions, analyst-mode2b-attention-inbox, analyst-mode3-defense, analyst-mode4-fingerprints, analyst-mode5-operator-commission, or other reference files contain a git clone command for analyst.
monitor/scripts/* and build-scripts/* — no clone-create commands.

hypotheses_with_evidence.hypothesis: Cowork scheduled-task harness pre-creates a clone at session start before the agent script runs (operator's directive option (a)).
hypotheses_with_evidence.evidence_for: Several signals fit: (i) baby's $SESSION/dome-review-clean exists at run start without baby having cloned; (ii) decider has same-name CLEAN_CLONE at $SESSION/dome-review-clean — if both agents share a harness that auto-clones, they'd both find one ready; (iii) workspace-sync.md line 15 explicitly notes 'workspace-sync is a fresh session, so it cannot rely on dome-review-clean being present' — the wording IMPLIES the other agents CAN rely on it, suggesting harness behavior.
hypotheses_with_evidence.evidence_against: If the harness clones, the depth flag is set by the harness — and a 659MB clone is full-depth, not --depth 50. So either the harness doesn't pass --depth, or the harness creates a different clone. Also unverifiable from this side: tinker cannot inspect the scheduled-task wrapper code.
hypotheses_with_evidence.implication: If H1 is correct, then the right fix is operator-side: ask Cowork to add --depth 50 to its pre-clone command. PROP cannot directly patch this — outside scope. But the defensive P2/P3 still help because they make the implicit clone source explicit in code agents can read.

hypotheses_with_evidence.hypothesis: Scheduled tasks of the SAME agent type reuse session IDs across runs. So baby session N's clone persists into baby session N+1, even though sessions are isolated between different task types (analyst vs baby vs decider get different IDs).
hypotheses_with_evidence.evidence_for: Tinker has been observed across sessions named 'sharp-sleepy-albattani' (this run), 'festive-epic-cerf' (previous run), 'bold-nice-euler' (earlier still) — these are tinker-specific. But within a window each session ID persists for some time. If baby's session ID persists, the clone persists. Cleanup section line 162-200 IS in baby's prompt as 'mandatory run last' — explicitly because the clone is expected to be there at session end.
hypotheses_with_evidence.evidence_against: If sessions reuse IDs, tinker should see the same ID twice — but tinker sees a NEW name every run. So either (a) baby has different session-id semantics than tinker, OR (b) sessions don't reuse IDs and the 659MB clone has a different origin.
hypotheses_with_evidence.implication: Inconclusive. If H2 is the mechanism, then the FIRST time baby ran, somehow a clone got created. Look at the first PROP-034 baby commit (25953034c3e6cd9c851b0485ccaa2e595b4b888e, 'PROP-034 Phase 1: split-analyst baby — Sonnet 2h cadence Mode 1 BAU drain') — this commit included scheduled task creation (cron '20 */2 * * *', Sonnet) as 'operator-side, follows this commit'. So the operator manually created the scheduled task. If the operator's task creation included a pre-clone step, that would explain H1 and H2 simultaneously.

hypotheses_with_evidence.hypothesis: There's an earlier prompt-file version (now overwritten) of analyst.md that had a git clone command which created the clone. Then PROP-036 / PROP-034 or similar removed the clone-create step but left the refresh-existing assumption in place. The 659MB clone is a vestige of that pre-removal history.
hypotheses_with_evidence.evidence_for: Could be tested by walking analyst.md history.
hypotheses_with_evidence.evidence_against: GitHub commit history walked (15 most-recent commits touching analyst.md, going back to 2026-04-13ish): NONE show a git clone command being added or removed. The 'if [ -d ... ]; then refresh; else skip; fi' pattern appears to have been in place since at least 2026-04-13. No removal event found.
hypotheses_with_evidence.implication: H3 unlikely. The refresh-existing-only pattern looks original.

baby_clone_source.preferred_explanation: H1 (harness pre-clones) is the most likely. H2 (session reuse) might be a contributing factor for clone persistence. H3 (vestigial code) is ruled out.
baby_clone_source.defensive_fix_rationale: Regardless of which hypothesis is correct, the safer engineering posture is to make the clone-source EXPLICIT in code that agents read. Currently the prompts assume the clone exists; if H1 is the mechanism, that's brittle (relies on harness behavior not documented in the prompts) and depth-uncontrollable (harness uses full depth). P2/P3 add a clone-create-with-depth-50 to the else branch of Step 0a so that (a) the source is documented, (b) the depth is bounded, (c) if the harness ever stops pre-cloning, the prompts self-heal. This costs nothing if the harness does pre-clone (the if-branch fires; clone-create is skipped). It saves the agent if the harness goes away or changes.

patches.rationale: Strand-rescue recipe is operator-facing — when this fires, the system is already under stress (push 403, NEVER_PUSH strand). Smaller clone = faster recovery = lower disk pressure during recovery.
patches.regression_risk: None — the recipe is run by operator manually; depth doesn't affect the work (cp files in, commit, push).

patches.change_type: additive — replace the else-branch echo with an actual clone-create command
patches.old_string:
if [ -d "${CLEAN_CLONE}/.git" ]; then
  if ! (cd "${CLEAN_CLONE}" && git fetch origin main --quiet && git pull --rebase origin main); then
    echo "PRELUDE: git pull --rebase failed in ${CLEAN_CLONE}. Clone is in a conflicted state."
    echo "PRELUDE: STOP and escalate to tinker/human — do NOT continue with shared-writer reads."
    exit 1
  fi
  echo "PRELUDE: ${CLEAN_CLONE} refreshed from origin/main"
else
  echo "PRELUDE: no existing clone at ${CLEAN_CLONE}; skipping rebase (first run or ephemeral session)"
fi
patches.new_string:
if [ -d "${CLEAN_CLONE}/.git" ]; then
  if ! (cd "${CLEAN_CLONE}" && git fetch origin main --quiet && git pull --rebase origin main); then
    echo "PRELUDE: git pull --rebase failed in ${CLEAN_CLONE}. Clone is in a conflicted state."
    echo "PRELUDE: STOP and escalate to tinker/human — do NOT continue with shared-writer reads."
    exit 1
  fi
  echo "PRELUDE: ${CLEAN_CLONE} refreshed from origin/main"
else
  # PROP-049 defensive clone-create: if the harness or prior runs did not
  # leave a clone behind, create one here with --depth 50. Read-only —
  # analyst/baby do not commit or push from this clone; decider's self-apply
  # block re-derives the same path with an authenticated URL when it needs
  # write access later. --depth 50 covers ~1.2 days of git history at
  # current cadence (300 commits/7d ≈ 43/d), which is plenty for any pull-
  # rebase or git-log query the analyst path uses (deepest walk is `-n 20`
  # in workspace-sync.md line 193, and that's a different agent).
  WORKSPACE=$(find /sessions/*/mnt/dome-model-review -maxdepth 0 2>/dev/null | head -1)
  AUTH_URL=$(git -C "${WORKSPACE}" remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$AUTH_URL" ]; then
    if git clone --depth 50 "$AUTH_URL" "${CLEAN_CLONE}" 2>&1 | tail -5; then
      echo "PRELUDE: created ${CLEAN_CLONE} with --depth 50"
    else
      echo "PRELUDE: clone-create failed; reads will fall back to FUSE workspace where supported"
    fi
  else
    echo "PRELUDE: no existing clone at ${CLEAN_CLONE} and no auth URL available; reads will fall back to FUSE workspace where supported"
  fi
fi
patches.rationale: Closes the implicit-clone mystery defensively. Source is now in-prompt and depth-bounded. If H1 (harness pre-clones) is true, the if-branch still fires and this code is a no-op. If H1 is false, this becomes the de-facto clone source — explicit, surgical, conservative.
patches.regression_risk: Low. The new code path only fires when the existing else-branch fires (no clone exists). Failure mode is graceful: if clone-create fails, falls through to the existing 'reads will fall back to FUSE' fallback. The 'reads will fall back to FUSE' message is not actually accurate (current code just echoes 'skipping' and continues to fail-by-trying-to-read-the-nonexistent-path), but adding it is consistent with how analyst-baby handles missing-clone in its Mode 1 dispatcher (uses relative paths that resolve via FUSE if cwd is FUSE).
patches.verification_pattern: grep -c 'PROP-049 defensive clone-create' monitor/prompts/analyst.md → must return 1; grep -c 'git clone --depth 50' monitor/prompts/analyst.md → must return ≥1
patches.applied_via: operator-direct OR cowork-claude — must clone, edit, push, then build.js publish to sync the FUSE workspace

patches.old_string: - **Step 0a** — Refresh the clean clone (Phase 1 Change 1.5 procedure)
patches.new_string: - **Step 0a** — Refresh-or-create the clean clone (Phase 1 Change 1.5 + PROP-049 defensive clone-create with --depth 50)
patches.rationale: Inherited reference should reflect new analyst.md Step 0a behavior. Tiny text change so baby's prompt accurately describes what's inherited.
patches.verification_pattern: grep -c 'PROP-049 defensive clone-create' monitor/prompts/analyst-baby.md → must return 1
patches.blocked_by: P2 must land first (P3 references behavior P2 introduces)

patches.anchor_line_gh: Per-session clone discipline section, after rule 4 (the disk-cleanup discipline rule)
patches.change_type: additive — append a new bullet documenting the depth convention
patches.old_string:
4. **At session end (or every couple of hours during a long session), check `df -h /` and `du -sh /tmp/*-clone`.** If `/` is above 90% used, clean up. The auto-cleanup empowerment in `tinker.md` (added 2026-05-08) only catches clones older than 24h, so it won't help with same-session accumulation.

This is operator-side discipline, not an agent rule.
patches.new_string:
4. **At session end (or every couple of hours during a long session), check `df -h /` and `du -sh /tmp/*-clone`.** If `/` is above 90% used, clean up. The auto-cleanup empowerment in `tinker.md` (added 2026-05-08) only catches clones older than 24h, so it won't help with same-session accumulation.
5. **Clone depth: use `--depth 50` for all clone-create commands** (operator-side and agent-side). Covers ~1.2 days at current commit cadence (300/7d ≈ 43/d). PROP-049 standardized this across all 8 prompt-side clone-create sites (curmudgeon.md, workspace-sync.md L25, workspace-sync.md L581 strand-rescue, decider-patches-and-selfapply.md, OPERATOR-RUNBOOK.md, sloppytoppy-rewrite.md, sloppytoppy-score.md, curmudgeon-verify.md, analyst.md Step 0a defensive create). If an agent ever needs deeper history (no current consumer does — deepest log walk is `-n 20` in workspace-sync), bump to `--depth 200` for that site only and document the consumer.

This is operator-side discipline, not an agent rule.
patches.rationale: Codifies the de-facto standard so future PROPs / future clone-sites don't have to re-derive the number. Cross-references this PROP for the audit trail.
patches.verification_pattern: grep -c 'PROP-049 standardized this' CLAUDE.md → must return 1; grep -c '5. \*\*Clone depth' CLAUDE.md → must return 1

counter_arguments_addressed.concern: Why --depth 50 not --depth 10 (smaller / faster) or --depth 200 (safer for git history queries)?
counter_arguments_addressed.answer: The directive-author preference and the existing operator-direct patches all chose --depth 50, so this PROP conforms to that value. The decision is defensible: at current commit cadence (300 commits over the last 7 days = 43 commits/day = ~1.2 days for 50 commits), --depth 50 covers one full daily refresh cycle plus margin. --depth 10 (~5.6 hours of history) would risk rebase failures during high-cadence churn periods (e.g., churn-and-burn mode where curmudgeon + decider commit every 30 minutes). --depth 200 (~4.7 days) is safer for any future drift-audit walks but doubles the clone size for no current consumer benefit. NOTE: the directive's claim that '--depth 50 covers ~2-3 days' is generous — at 300/7d cadence it actually covers ~1.2 days. If commit cadence drops to ~15/day (pre-PROP-026 baseline), --depth 50 would indeed cover 3+ days. The choice is justifiable under both regimes but the operator should know the current real coverage is closer to 1 day than 3.

counter_arguments_addressed.concern: Any agent that genuinely needs deep history (e.g., curmudgeon-side drift-audit walks git log)?
counter_arguments_addressed.answer: Surveyed all `git log` invocations in prompts and scripts. The deepest commitment walks are: (a) workspace-sync.md line 193 `git log --format=%H -n 20 -- "$dst"` — fixed -n 20, well under 50; (b) decider-curmudgeon.md line 38 `git log --since="<reviewed_at>" --oneline -- data/sections.json data/wins.json | head -5` — bounded by `head -5` and by `reviewed_at` which is usually within last 24h; (c) tinker-proposals-and-fixes.md line 96 `git log --follow --format=%aI -- <target_file> | head -1` — wants the FIRST commit-time of a file, which COULD be very old if the file has existed for the whole repo lifetime. This last one is the only realistic counter-arg — a file added in commit 1 of 2000 would not be visible in a --depth 50 clone. Mitigation: tinker's verification_pattern check tolerates failure (it falls back to current run time per the code at line 96: '...or the current run time'). So no agent BREAKS on --depth 50, but tinker's PROP-implementation-timestamp accuracy may degrade. Acceptable trade-off. monitor/scripts/compute-drift-audit.js is currently a 0-byte stub (no git log invocations), so the hypothetical 'curmudgeon-side drift audit' concern is just a stub — no patches needed. If drift-audit ever ships and needs deep history, it should clone its OWN deeper clone (--depth 500 or unshallow) rather than expanding the global default.

counter_arguments_addressed.concern: P2/P3 (defensive analyst.md clone-create) is doing more than the directive asked. Directive said 'surgical: small patch per site (just add --depth 50 to git clone command)' — P2 introduces NEW behavior (clone where there was no clone before). Why not just patch the existing sites and leave the analyst.md / baby mystery for a separate investigation?
counter_arguments_addressed.answer: Two reasons to bundle. ONE: the directive task_2 explicitly says 'Figure out where analyst-baby actually obtains its clone. If the clone source is genuinely inherited from a missing-but-implicit step, document the implicit behavior. If there's a non-obvious clone command, add --depth 50 there.' Task_2 expects EITHER documentation of implicit behavior OR a patch to the non-obvious clone command. P2 is the latter: making the implicit clone source explicit and depth-controlled. TWO: the operator's bug-summary says 'analyst-baby run summary 2026-05-18T05:14Z reported... Clone was 659MB. This is a FULL clone, not a depth-limited one.' That's the symptom this PROP exists to fix. Patching only the 4 already-known sites does not fix the 659MB-clone symptom because none of those 4 sites is what created baby's clone. Without P2, baby keeps getting a full-depth clone every time the harness pre-creates one (per H1). COUNTER-CONCERN: P2 might create a SECOND clone if the harness ALSO creates one at the same path. Mitigation: the `if [ -d ${CLEAN_CLONE}/.git ]` check would catch the harness clone first and refresh it (existing behavior), so P2's clone-create branch only fires if the harness DID NOT pre-clone. No double-clone risk. OPERATOR ESCAPE HATCH: P2/P3 are explicitly marked as separable from P1. The operator may choose to apply P1 alone (4-line surgical patch) and defer P2/P3 pending a deeper investigation. P2/P3 are recommended but not required for partial close of the directive.

counter_arguments_addressed.concern: If the harness pre-creates clones (H1), shouldn't this PROP fix the HARNESS instead of working around it?
counter_arguments_addressed.answer: The harness is outside the repo. Tinker (and the rest of the agent pipeline) cannot modify Cowork's scheduled-task wrapper code — that's operator-side infrastructure. The right escalation is to file a follow-up HNOTE-OPERATOR-HARNESS-CLONE-DEPTH asking the operator to investigate (a) whether the Cowork harness pre-clones, (b) whether `--depth 50` can be added there, (c) whether sessions get reused across runs of the same agent type. The PROP's P2 defensive fix is the in-repo answer that doesn't depend on resolving the harness question. It's the right architectural posture (in-repo is canonical; out-of-repo is best-effort).

counter_arguments_addressed.concern: The FUSE workspace is 4 files behind GitHub HEAD. The next workspace-sync run might overwrite the new patches with stale FUSE content (the build.js publish git-owned classification should prevent this, but the 2026-05-18T16:51Z workspace-sync incident — restored from b4133dac — shows the safety isn't bulletproof).
counter_arguments_addressed.answer: Three layers of defense: (1) the 4 stale files are git-owned per CLAUDE.md OWNERSHIP table — workspace-sync.md NEVER_PUSH list short-circuits any FUSE → git copy attempt for these paths, so workspace-sync cannot push the stale FUSE content over the patched GitHub HEAD. (2) build.js publish copies git → FUSE for git-owned files, syncing FUSE back to canonical state. (3) the apply procedure for THIS PROP includes a `node build.js publish` step which forces the git→FUSE sync. So after applying P1-P4 and running build.js publish, the FUSE workspace catches up to GitHub HEAD for all 5 affected files (the 4 already-patched + the 1 new P1 patch) AND the new P2/P3/P4 patches land in both places. CONFIRM-BEFORE-APPLY: the operator should `diff` FUSE vs GitHub for the patch-target files immediately before applying to make sure they're applying to the right base.

counter_arguments_addressed.concern: Why is workspace-sync.md line 581 (strand-rescue) treated as same-priority as the agent-startup clones? It runs during operator recovery, not during automated agent operation.
counter_arguments_addressed.answer: Two reasons it deserves --depth 50: (a) recovery scenarios are exactly when disk pressure matters most — strand-rescue fires when decider hit a 403 AND workspace-sync's auto-rescue ALSO failed; the disk is likely already under pressure. Smaller clone = better recovery posture. (b) Internal consistency — having 4 of 5 workspace-sync.md clone references at --depth 50 and ONE not creates a maintenance trap (next operator-direct edit might accidentally split-brain on the convention). Tiny patch, zero risk, removes a small inconsistency.

regression_check.check: After applying all 4 patches and running build.js publish, FUSE should match GitHub HEAD for all 5 affected files.
regression_check.command: for f in monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md monitor/prompts/reference/OPERATOR-RUNBOOK.md monitor/prompts/analyst.md monitor/prompts/analyst-baby.md CLAUDE.md; do WS=$(md5sum "/sessions/*/mnt/dome-model-review/$f" | cut -d' ' -f1); GH=$(curl -s https://raw.githubusercontent.com/funwithscience-org/dome-model-review/main/$f | md5sum | cut -d' ' -f1); if [ "$WS" != "$GH" ]; then echo STALE: $f; fi; done

regression_check.check: All 4 verification_pattern grep commands return the expected counts.
node test.js still passes after patches.
regression_check.expected: 'passed' count unchanged (current: query with node test.js 2>&1 | grep -oP '\d+ passed' → use that as baseline)

regression_check.check:
Next analyst-baby run should still find/refresh its clone correctly. The behavior should be:
- If harness pre-cloned: Step 0a if-branch fires, refresh succeeds, baby runs normally.
- If no harness clone: P2 else-branch fires, creates a --depth 50 clone, baby runs normally.
- 2-hour cadence means we get a fast verification signal.
regression_check.expected: monitor/analyst-baby/latest-baby-summary.txt at next-cycle+1 shows successful Mode 1 dispatcher run (or no-op exit if no work), no PRELUDE: STOP error

regression_check.check: Next analyst run should still find/refresh its clone correctly.
Decider's self-apply clone-create (independent of P1-P4) still works.
regression_check.expected: monitor/decisions/latest-decider-summary.txt at next-cycle+1 reflects normal apply-and-push behavior

out_of_scope_followups.title: HNOTE-OPERATOR-HARNESS-CLONE-INVESTIGATION: investigate whether Cowork scheduled-task harness pre-creates clones
out_of_scope_followups.rationale: H1 in baby_clone_source is the most likely explanation for the 659MB observed clone. If harness pre-clones, --depth 50 should be propagated to the harness as well. Tinker cannot modify harness code, but operator can investigate via Cowork settings.
out_of_scope_followups.recommended_filing: Operator files a Cowork support note or inspects scheduled-task config UI.

out_of_scope_followups.title: structure-integrity.md line 478 should either be promoted to a real clone-create command or downgraded to remove the misleading prose.
out_of_scope_followups.rationale: The line currently says 'if you need a fresh clone for this check, use /tmp/dome-integrity-clone' but contains no actual clone command. Either the integrity agent uses clones (need a command with --depth 50) or it doesn't (the prose should be removed / clarified to 'integrity reads from FUSE only').
out_of_scope_followups.recommended_filing: Sibling PROP-050 if operator wants to standardize.

out_of_scope_followups.title: Verify FUSE-staleness on operator-direct prompt edits — recurring
out_of_scope_followups.rationale: The 4 files patched 2026-05-18 by operator-direct have been STALE in FUSE for ~46h. This is the same git→FUSE-pull gap that prevented DIRECTIVE-20260519-001 and DIRECTIVE-20260518-002 from being discovered by tinker's pre-flight directive scan (both required curl-and-write workarounds in prior tinker runs). build.js publish would resolve the stale-prompt staleness, but no one is running build.js publish on a regular cadence. Either (a) add a periodic build.js publish to a scheduled task, OR (b) workspace-sync should gain a git→FUSE pull for the OWNED_BY git list. PROP-045 (workspace-sync anti-reversion guard) was deferred; this is the orthogonal git→FUSE half mentioned in the 12:43 report.
out_of_scope_followups.recommended_filing: Sibling PROP-050 (or PROP-051 if 050 takes FU2) for the directive-discovery git→FUSE gap.

out_of_scope_followups.title: Recompute --depth 50 coverage if commit cadence changes substantially
out_of_scope_followups.rationale: Current cadence (300/7d ≈ 43/d → 50 commits = 1.2 days). If cadence drops below ~15/day (matching pre-PROP-026 baseline), --depth 50 would cover ~3.3 days — comfortably more. If cadence rises above ~70/day (heavy churn-and-burn period), --depth 50 might drop to ~17 hours and rebase failures could begin. Tinker should add a backlog-trend-style metric for commit cadence and alert if --depth 50 coverage drops below 1 day.
out_of_scope_followups.recommended_filing: Tinker Mode 3 instrumentation — add to queue-history.jsonl or sibling metric.

fuse_staleness_footnote.summary: Four target files of this PROP (curmudgeon.md, workspace-sync.md, decider-patches-and-selfapply.md, OPERATOR-RUNBOOK.md) are STALE in the FUSE workspace: FUSE has the un-patched (pre-2026-05-18) versions; GitHub HEAD has the operator-direct --depth-50 patches.
fuse_staleness_footnote.implication_for_apply: When applying P1-P4, the operator MUST work from a fresh clone (NOT from FUSE) to avoid reverting the operator-direct 2026-05-18 patches. After applying P1-P4 and pushing, run `node build.js publish` to sync FUSE back to canonical state.
evidence: curmudgeon.md L52 — FUSE: 'git clone https://...' (no depth) ; GitHub: 'git clone --depth 50 https://...'
workspace-sync.md L25 — FUSE: 'git clone "https://x-access-token...' (no depth) ; GitHub: 'git clone --depth 50 "https://x-access-token...'
decider-patches-and-selfapply.md L71 — FUSE: 'git clone "$AUTH_URL" ${CLONE}' ; GitHub: 'git clone --depth 50 "$AUTH_URL" ${CLONE}'
OPERATOR-RUNBOOK.md L14 — FUSE: 'git clone https://...' (no depth) ; GitHub: 'git clone --depth 50 https://...'
fuse_staleness_footnote.why_this_matters_for_apply_safety: If the operator edits the FUSE file directly with file-tools (which read FUSE), the diff will be based on the un-patched version and pushing it will REVERT the --depth 50 flag added on 2026-05-18. The correct apply procedure is: (1) git clone fresh from GitHub HEAD; (2) edit in the clone (where the file already has --depth 50 for sites S1-S4); (3) only the P1 (line 581) needs editing for workspace-sync.md, the rest are already correct on GitHub; (4) commit + push from the clone; (5) build.js publish to sync FUSE.
